item.title: South Korean Finance Minister Says Growth to Exceed 2% This Year
item.description: South Korean Finance Minister Koo Yun Cheol said he expects the country’s economic growth to exceed 2% this year, supported by surging global demand for semiconductors.

item.title: Asian Currencies Slide as Crude Jumps on Middle East Standoff
item.description: Emerging Asian currencies were lower, with the Korean won and the Thai baht leading losses amid a stalemate in the Middle East talks to end the 10-week-long US-Iran conflict.

item.title: Blackstone Agrees to Buy Stake in Greek Online Market Skroutz
item.description: Blackstone Inc. has agreed to acquire a majority stake in Greek online market place Skroutz from CVC Capital Partners Plc, expanding its footprint in the Mediterranean country.

item.title: Hedge Funds Make Their Move as Litigation Finance Assets Slump
item.description: A losing streak in litigation finance is attracting hedge funds and other alternative investment managers looking to invest in legal claims at distressed valuations, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: China’s Private Refiners Seek Beijing Approval to Cut Run Rates
item.description: China’s private refiners have sought government approval to cut oil-processing rates, a month after Beijing ordered them to produce at any cost in order to secure domestic fuel availability.

item.title: UK Bond Yields Rise on Higher Oil Price, Political Risks
Prosus Sells 5% Stake in Delivery Hero to Activist Investor
item.description: Prosus NV is selling a 5% stake in Delivery Hero SE to Aspex Management, the second-largest shareholder of the German e-commerce firm that has been pushing its chief executive officer to offload more assets or leave the company.

item.title: Emerging Stocks Set to Close at Record High on Tech Bets
item.description: Emerging-market equities rose on AI trades as investors brushed off concerns over stalled peace talks between the US and Iran.

item.title: Kiwi May Defy Hedge-Fund Bears on Hawkish RBNZ, Analysts Say
item.description: New Zealand’s dollar may defy bearish hedge-fund positioning and rally in the near-term on a hawkish tilt from the central bank and any de-escalation in the Middle East, analysts say.

item.title: Pictet Fund Plows 30% of Cash Into AI Stocks on Risk Revival
item.description: A $3.5 billion multi-asset fund at Pictet Asset Management has sharply raised its equity exposure, shifting as much as 30% of its cash-equivalent holdings into artificial-intelligence heavyweights across Asia and the US.

item.title: Bessent’s Unprecedented Grasp on Japanese Policy Tests Takaichi
item.description: When a major meltdown in Japanese bonds spilled over into US Treasuries during Scott Bessent’s high-profile visit to Davos in January, he had more than a few words for his Japanese counterpart.

item.title: Blackstone’s Gray Says Alignment Helps Calm Private Credit Angst
item.description: A recent wave of investor redemption requests across the $1.8 trillion market for private credit prompted Blackstone Inc. to enlist senior executives in putting up capital to bolster its flagship fund, according to President Jon Gray.

item.title: Copper Shrugs Off Middle East Uncertainty to Aim for Record High
item.description: Copper headed for its highest-ever close and other metals advanced as traders shrugged off the apparent deadlock between the US and Iran to join a broader rally for risk assets.

item.title: BYD Sees Annual Sales Rise Despite Tepid Market, Says JPMorgan
item.description: BYD Co. is projecting an increase in annual sales, fueled by a more optimistic domestic outlook and an aggressive global expansion that aims to defy cooling demand in the broader electric vehicle market, said JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: Goldman Sees Rates Pressure as AI Fuels K-Shaped Korea, Taiwan
item.description: South Korea and Taiwan’s artificial intelligence-fueled chip booms are set to swell both economies’ trade surpluses and pressure their central banks to raise interest rates later this year.

item.title: RBI Should Dial Back Rupee Intervention, Former Governor Says
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India should temper its currency intervention efforts, and continue with measures to open up the nation’s markets, a former governor said, about a month after the monetary authority imposed sweeping curbs to support a struggling rupee.

item.title: Modi Asks Indians to Stop Buying Gold, Hitting Jewelry Stocks
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has called on Indians to avoid buying gold for at least a year to preserve foreign-exchange reserves, a surprising appeal in a country where the metal plays a vital role in savings, weddings and religious festivals.

item.title: Malaysia Pension Fund KWAP Taps Ex-Banker Jay Khairil as New CEO
item.description: Malaysia state pension fund Kumpulan Wang Persaraan Diperbadankan has appointed former banker Jay Khairil Jeremy Abdullah as its new chief executive officer effective May 20, it said in a statement on Monday.

item.title: ECB to Hike Rates Twice in 2026 as Inflation Jumps, Survey Shows
item.description: The European Central Bank will raise interest rates twice this year as the Iran war drives inflation higher, a Bloomberg survey showed.

item.title: Indonesia Central Bank Bills Outstanding Rise Most in Two Years
item.description: The total amount of Indonesian central bank bills outstanding climbed by the most in almost two years last month as the monetary authority sought to attract capital inflows to support the weakening rupiah.

item.title: South Korea Exploring Using Hyundai Robots as Army Numbers Fall
item.description: South Korea’s military is exploring a strategic partnership with Hyundai Motor Co. to potentially deploy robotics to the front lines as Seoul accelerates investment in AI-powered, unmanned systems to tackle a deepening troop shortage.

item.title: Chips Have an Earnings Season of Their Own
item.description: It’s impossible to argue with a showing this strong.

item.title: Race for Critical Minerals Leaves EU Struggling to Keep Up
item.description: Amid a hotly contested global race for minerals, a mining project in Slovakia illustrates the EU’s struggle to get its act together.

item.title: ECB’s Guindos Urges Rate ‘Prudence’ With War to Hit Growth, FT Reports
item.description: The European Central Bank should be cautious when considering raising interest rates as the full impact of the Iran war on economic expansion is still to be felt, Vice President Luis de Guindos told the Financial Times.

item.title: Africa’s CEOs Warn Online Gambling Is Draining Customer Wallets
item.description: Africa’s growing gambling use is sapping income that would ordinarily be flowing into groceries, entertainment and mobile phone bills.

item.title: Iron Ore Climbs as Steady China Demand Offsets Supply Risks
item.description: Iron ore rose as signs of solid Chinese demand offset concerns over rising supply, with futures hitting the highest since October 2024.

item.title: China’s Zhaojin Mining Eyes Overseas Gold Assets as M&A Heats Up
item.description: China’s Zhaojin Mining Industry Co. is looking to acquire more gold mines in Africa and other regions, Chief Investment Officer Xu Jianzhuo said in an interview.

item.title: China April Auto Sales Drop as War Hits Demand for Gasoline Cars
item.description: China’s car sales fell 21.5% in April after deliveries of gasoline vehicles plunged due to the Iran oil shock, and electric car demand wasn’t strong enough to counter the slump.

item.title: SoftBank Plans to Make Large-Scale Batteries for AI Data Centers
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp.’s mobile unit said it plans to begin large-scale battery cell manufacturing at its Sakai, Osaka plant to address growing power demand for AI services.

item.title: Floundering Rupee May Keep Indian Equities on a Tight Leash
item.description: Monex Europe expects the rupee to hit 98 per dollar by year-end.
